title: J. M. Meulenhoff
content:
{{Top|rwc}}
'''Uitgeverij J. M. Meulenhoff''' ('''*M=''sf''X'''), known simply as '''J. M. Meulenhoff''' and '''Meulenhoff''', is an [[Wikipedia:Amsterdam|Amsterdam]]-based publishing company founded on [[September 15]], [[1800s#1895|1895]] by Johannes Marius Meulenhoff.<ref name="Founding">{{WebCite|url=http://meulenhoff.nl/nl/p4cd00a2d37d78/oprichter-jm-meulenhoff.html|text="Biografie van J. M. Meulenhoff"|language=Dutch|archivedate=20160408115907}}</ref>

J. M. Meulenhoff publishes [[Wikipedia:Dutch language|Dutch]] and foreign translations of novels, poetry, essays, and non-fiction. The company was responsible for printing the Dutch translations of ''[[Star Wars]]'' novels after [[A. W. Bruna]] stopped publishing them. In [[2002]], the crime, science-fiction, and fantasy sections of Meulenhoff joined with the science-fiction and fantasy section of Het Spectrum to form [[Uitgeverij M]], which took over the printing of the ''Star Wars'' titles.
